name: Serra de Segària PR-CV 415 Loop
name_original: La Serra de Segaría — Running circular desde Benimeli
description: This difficult 8.0-mile (13.0 km) circular jogging route from Benimeli offers panoramic views and varied terrain in Parc Públic de Segària.
region_intro: Jogging around Benimeli offers diverse landscapes, from the foothills of the Sierra de Segària mountain range to the fertile "Rectoria" valley. The region features varied terrain, including mountain trails with significant elevation changes and serene agricultural paths. Benimeli's proximity to the coast also provides access to routes along the Mediterranean Sea. This blend of mountainous, valley, and coastal environments makes it an appealing destination for runners of all levels. Best jogging routes around Benimeli The most popular jogging route is Serra de Segària PR-CV 415 Loop, an 8.0 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the surrounding valley and the Mediterranean Sea. Another top favourite among local runners is Jaciment Romà de l'Almadrava loop from Desembocadura del Riu Racons, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This route features mostly paved surfaces near the coast, passing historical archaeological remains. Local runners also love the Almadraba Beach loop from Yamasaki, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes with views of the sea, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Jogging around Benimeli is defined by mountain ranges, fertile valleys, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain ascents to gentle coastal runs. The routes in Benimeli are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Benimeli's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Benimeli?
A: There are over 200 running routes around Benimeli, offering a wide variety of options for all fitness levels. These include trails through the Sierra de Segària mountains, the fertile "Rectoria" valley, and along the coast.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Benimeli?
A: Jogging routes in Benimeli feature diverse terrain. You'll find challenging mountain trails with significant elevation changes in the Sierra de Segària, serene paths through agricultural landscapes filled with orange and almond trees in the "Rectoria" valley, and mostly paved, moderate routes along the nearby coast.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Benimeli?
A: Yes, Benimeli offers options for beginners. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are 18 easy running trails available. These typically feature gentler inclines and less challenging surfaces, perfect for a relaxed jog.

Q: Can I find challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Benimeli provides 50 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872733722/serra-de-segaria-pr-cv-415-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Segària PR-CV 415 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is an 8-mile trail with significant elevation changes, offering panoramic views and a demanding workout.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Benimeli's running routes are rich with natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the impressive Sierra de Segària mountain range, discover natural springs like El Campillo, Ramón, and Catalá, and even encounter archaeological remains, including an Iberian site and Moorish terraces on trails like the Monte de Segaria. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5407844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is also accessible for birdwatching and scenic runs through its 'paddy fields'.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes, especially those in the Sierra de Segària, provide stunning panoramic views. On clear days, you can see the surrounding valley, the Mediterranean Sea, and even Montgó in the distance. The Segaria Viewpoint, located in the central part of the old Moorish castle, also offers strategic views of the area.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Benimeli?
A: The running routes in Benimeli are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to gentle coastal runs, and the overall quality of the trails.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Benimeli offers several circular running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31941474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaciment Romà de l'Almadrava loop from Desembocadura del Riu Racons&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate coastal path that forms a loop, passing historical archaeological remains. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21939091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadraba Beach loop from Yamasaki&lt;/a&gt;, which also provides a circular coastal experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Benimeli?
A: Benimeli enjoys a mild climate, making it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Warm summers and gentle winters mean you can find comfortable conditions for your runs, though early mornings or late evenings are recommended during the peak summer months to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any running routes near the coast or beaches?
A: Yes, Benimeli's proximity to the coast allows access to several coastal running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33810527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Molinell River – Beachfront Promenade in Spain loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21939091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadraba Beach loop from Yamasaki&lt;/a&gt; are excellent options for coastal jogs, offering mostly paved surfaces and sea views.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Indeed. The Monte de Segaria trail, accessible from Benimeli's Plaza Mayor, leads to significant historical archaeological remains, including an Iberian site and Moorish terraces. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31941474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaciment Romà de l'Almadrava loop&lt;/a&gt; also passes by historical archaeological remains near the coast.
tips: Medium difficulty is an understatement! We are in good shape and really fit, but this tour was difficult. Partly there were no paths, it was more of a climbing tour on the mountainside. Good shoes and a head for heights are a must!

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the La Serra de Segaria running circular from Benimeli?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Benimeli. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as the main access point for this trail.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as difficult for jogging. It features varied and challenging terrain, including alpine sections and karstic areas that require good fitness and sure-footedness. It's best suited for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse terrain, including mountain hiking paths, alpine sections, and some accessible paths. You'll also encounter short stretches of paved and asphalted roads, as well as unpaved and natural sections. A unique part of the route follows an old, disused irrigation canal. Be prepared for some scrambling and navigating very karstic terrain, especially when ascending to the summit.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers stunning panoramic views from the summit of Sierra de Segària, overlooking the Marjal de Pego-Oliva natural park, Montgó mountain, and the beaches of Denia. You'll also pass by significant caves like Cova Bolumini and Cova Fosca, and historical elements such as ancient wells and hydrological infrastructures. One notable highlight is the Dark Cave.

faqs.question: Is the La Serra de Segaria — Running circular from Benimeli trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs within Parc Públic de Segària are not detailed. Given the challenging terrain and potential for wildlife, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are fit enough for a difficult route. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access Parc Públic de Segària?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access Parc Públic de Segària for recreational activities like jogging. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this route is typically during the cooler months, such as spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November), to avoid the intense summer heat. These seasons also offer pleasant temperatures and often clearer views. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Serra de Segaría (72% of the route), la Segària de Benimeli (36%), and various segments of the SL-CV 109 and PR CV-109 trails.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this difficult jogging route?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Bring plenty of water, especially as there may not be water sources on the trail itself. A map or GPS device, sun protection, and a first-aid kit are also highly recommended. Layers of clothing are useful for changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat in Benimeli after the run?
faqs.answer: Benimeli is a small village, but you can usually find local establishments like cafes or bars where you can refuel and relax after completing your run. It's a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

faqs.question: What makes the Serra de Segaria mountain unique?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Segària is often referred to as 'the Sleeping Stone Giant' due to its distinctive appearance. Beyond its natural beauty, it holds significant cultural, historical, and tourist value, including remnants of an Iberian settlement and ancient hydrological infrastructures.
